title: acetosella euphorbioides
intro: Acetosella euphorbioides, also known as the Wood's Rose or Wood's Sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the family Oxalidaceae. It is native to the eastern United States and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves contain high levels of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Acetosella euphorbioides can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used as a natural dye and in traditional medicine, although it should be used with caution due to its high oxalic acid content.
